must: Forest Restoration and Wildfire Risk Mitigation
facts.src: The Forest Restoration and Wildfire Risk Mitigation (FRWRM) Grant Program
administered by the Colorado State Forest Service (CSFS), was established in 2017 through Senate Bill 17-050
The CSFS estimates that approximately $7,040,000 is available for the 2025-2026 grant cycle.
Up to $2 million may be awarded to Capacity Building projects
Applicants must demonstrate the ability to meet a minimum 1:1 match requirement, or 50% match.
eligible for a reduced match requirement of 25%, or a 3:1 grant to match ratio
volunteer labor (valued at current CSFS volunteer labor rates - $38.74/hour per individual for 2026)
Grant recipients will be required to submit spatial map data (e.g., shapefiles) with each reimbursement request
authorize CSFS access to the project site both before treatments occur and for at least two years post-treatment
the total federal acres must not exceed 50% of the total project acres
Hard copies will not be accepted.
